deep_dive.title: EML Trees Are Universal Approximators
deep_dive.abstract: The recently introduced EML (Exp-Minus-Log) function acts as continuous analogue of NAND gates, providing a compositional building block capable of representing elementary functions. In this work, we study the expressive power of tree-structured compositions of EML functions. We show that such trees enjoy a universal approximation property for functions in $W^{k, \infty}$ for $k \in \mathbb N$, drawing on classical neural network approximation arguments while exploiting the ability to explicitly construct EML trees that mimic polynomial representations. We further propose a learning algorithm for EML-type trees equipped with fitting parameters, and demonstrate its feasibility in practical optimization problems. Our results establish EML trees as a theoretically grounded framework for function approximation.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: EML trees for symbolic function approximation
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: This paper proves that compositions of EML functions (Exp-Minus-Log) form trees that are universal approximators for functions in Sobolev spaces, and proposes a learning algorithm for fitting such trees, demonstrating feasibility on optimization problems. The work provides a theoretically grounded framework for symbolic regression via tree-structured function representation.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文证明EML（指数-减去-对数）函数的树状组合能够普遍逼近Sobolev空间中的函数，并提出了一个针对EML树的学习算法，可调整参数以拟合数据。实验表明该算法在实际优化问题中可行。这项工作为通过树结构函数表示进行符号回归提供了理论基础的框架。

deep_dive.title: Toward a Systematic Understanding and Interactive Search of Lyapunov-Style Proofs in Optimization
deep_dive.abstract: Lyapunov-style convergence proofs, which establish a nonincreasing sequence to provide a quantitative convergence rate for an algorithm, are popular and often considered desirable in first-order optimization. However, existing approaches to finding such Lyapunov functions rely on hand-designed templates or prior insight on the proof structure, and do not certify that the resulting Lyapunov-style analysis provides the sharpest convergence bound. In this work, we introduce a systematic framework for converting a tight, analytic convergence proof of an optimization algorithm, often found via computer assistance, into an equivalent proof based on Lyapunov functions. We implement a concrete procedure that combines a performance estimation problem (PEP) toolbox with elementary linear algebra, and show that it captures a number of prior Lyapunov analyses within a single Jupyter notebook. Based on our implementation, a user can straightforwardly test our systematic and interactive procedure on their own optimization algorithm of interest to search for a tight Lyapunov-style proof via code, without the need to comprehend the implementation details. We extend the application of our framework and discover four novel analytic Lyapunov-style proofs, where notably, one of them identifies a new exact optimal proximal algorithm for strongly monotone inclusion problems.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: systematic framework for Lyapunov-style proofs in optimization
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 优化中李雅普诺夫风格证明的系统框架
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 优化中李雅普诺夫风格证明的系统框架
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 优化中李雅普诺夫风格证明的系统框架
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a systematic framework to convert tight analytic convergence proofs into Lyapunov-based proofs, enabling interactive search and sharpest bounds.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有李雅普诺夫函数寻找依赖手工模板，缺乏对最优性的保证。本文提出系统性框架，将紧致解析收敛证明自动转化为等效的李雅普诺夫证明，结合性能估计与计算机辅助搜索，实现交互式探索并确保得到最紧收敛界。该方法在优化算法分析中具有广泛适用性。

deep_dive.matched_query_text: Papers central to 请帮我查找动力系统的李雅普诺夫函数发现的相关论文，采用传统方法和transformer等ai算法都可以, especially work that connects or combines: discovering Lyapunov functions for dynamical systems; neural network based Lyapunov function; transformer model for Lyapunov function discovery; stability analysis of dynamical systems using Lyapunov functions; Lyapunov neural network methods; data-driven discovery of Lyapunov functions; recent advances in Lyapunov function discovery using deep learning; traditional methods for Lyapunov function construction; comparison of classical and AI methods for Lyapunov functions.

deep_dive.title: Learning to Adapt: Reptile-D-Learning for Robust and Efficient Control Under Parametric Uncertainty
deep_dive.abstract: Learning-based Lyapunov Control (LLC) provides formal stability guarantees for nonlinear systems, but its validity relies heavily on accurate system models. Parameter variations and uncertainties may invalidate stability constraints, leading to costly retraining. Although D-learning can estimate Lyapunov derivatives without relying on explicit dynamics models, it remains limited by single-task dynamics and degrades under large parameter shifts. We propose Reptile-D-learning, a framework that leverages the Reptile meta-learning algorithm to capture shared dynamical structures across systems with different parameters, thereby learning a generalizable Lyapunov network initialization and a high-performance controller. Experiments on multiple nonlinear control systems demonstrate that Reptile-D-learning significantly improves both generalization and rapid adaptation to unseen parameter configurations.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 基于神经网络的李雅普诺夫函数
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 基于神经网络的李雅普诺夫函数
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 基于神经网络的李雅普诺夫函数
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes Reptile-D-learning, a meta-learning framework for Lyapunov network initialization and control, enabling robust stability guarantees under parametric uncertainty without retraining.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对基于学习的李雅普诺夫控制对模型参数敏感的问题，本文提出Reptile-D-learning框架，利用Reptile元学习算法捕捉不同参数系统的共享动态结构，从而学习可泛化的李雅普诺夫网络初始化和高效控制器。实验表明该方法在参数大范围变化时仍能保持稳定性保证，避免了昂贵的重训练。该工作将元学习引入李雅普诺夫函数发现，推动了数据驱动稳定分析的实用化。

deep_dive.title: FactorLibrary: From Polynomials to Circuits via Recursive Subgoals
deep_dive.abstract: Finding minimal arithmetic circuits for polynomials over finite fields is a combinatorially hard problem central to algebraic complexity theory. We formulate it as a reinforcement learning problem in two directions, bottom-up and top-down. To address the challenge of a fast-growing combinatorial search space, we introduce FactorLibrary, which stores factorizable subexpressions that serve as reusable subgoals across training episodes. We trained a bottom-up agent with Gumbel-PPO-MCTS and two top-down agents with PPO+MCTS and SAC. The PPO+MCTS top-down agent exhibited the most stable performance, finding certified optimal circuits up to complexity $8$ with a success rate of $91.8\%$.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: reinforcement learning for symbolic expression discovery
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 强化学习用于符号表达式发现
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 强化学习用于符号表达式发现
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 强化学习用于符号表达式发现
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Formulates minimal arithmetic circuit discovery as a reinforcement learning problem, using a library of factorizable subexpressions as reusable subgoals. Achieves 91.8% success for optimal circuits up to complexity 8.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对有限域上多项式最小算术电路这个组合难题，将其建模为强化学习问题，并引入FactorLibrary存储可分解的子表达式作为可重用子目标。训练了多种强化学习智能体，其中PPO+MCTS自上而下智能体表现最稳定，在复杂度不超过8的电路上以91.8%的成功率找到认证最优解。该方法为符号表达式发现提供了强化学习新范式。

quick_skim.title: Differential Equation Inductive Robustness Axiomatization
quick_skim.abstract: This article establishes the completeness of an axiomatization for the robust safety of dynamical systems with polynomial differential equations on bounded time horizons. Safety properties of robust systems are uniformly reduced to a sound axiomatization of polynomial invariants, resulting in reliable logical proofs of correctness. Approximate decidability results are also established: there is a computable algorithm such that, given any perturbation parameter $δ$, it either produces a symbolic proof of robust safety (hence correctly decides the dynamical system to be robustly safe), or correctly decides that the system is not robustly safe under a perturbation of level $δ$. In contrast to earlier works, this article crucially leverages results from subanalytic geometry to retain a level of exactness, thereby establishing positive results of provability/decidability allowing for arbitrary bounded (semialgebraic) initial/post conditions even without positive separation at their (topological) boundaries. This enables the generation of proofs of inductive safety beyond finite time horizons for general hybrid dynamical systems.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: robust safety of polynomial dynamical systems via axiomatization, related to Lyapunov stability analysis
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 通过公理化方法证明多项式动力系统的鲁棒安全性，与Lyapunov稳定性分析相关
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 通过公理化方法证明多项式动力系统的鲁棒安全性，与Lyapunov稳定性分析相关
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 通过公理化方法证明多项式动力系统的鲁棒安全性，与Lyapunov稳定性分析相关
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This paper completes an axiomatization for robust safety of polynomial differential equations, providing logical proofs and approximate decidability, with implications for Lyapunov-like stability verification.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文建立了多项式微分方程鲁棒安全性的完备公理化系统，将安全性质归约为多项式不变式的可靠逻辑证明。文章还给出了近似可判定性结果：对于任意扰动参数，算法要么生成鲁棒安全的符号证明，要么判定系统在该扰动下不安全。这一工作为动力系统的形式化验证提供了新工具，尤其适用于Lyapunov稳定性分析等安全关键场景。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文建立了多项式微分方程鲁棒安全性的完备公理化系统，将安全性质归约为多项式不变式的可靠逻辑证明。文章还给出了近似可判定性结果：对于任意扰动参数，算法要么生成鲁棒安全的符号证明，要么判定系统在该扰动下不安全。这一工作为动力系统的形式化验证提供了新工具，尤其适用于Lyapunov稳定性分析等安全关键场景。

quick_skim.title: Weight Adaptation for Improving Parallel Performance of Adaptive Stochastic Natural Gradient
quick_skim.abstract: Probabilistic model-based evolutionary algorithms are promising for black-box optimization. Specifically, the adaptive stochastic natural gradient (ASNG) adaptively updates its learning rate, a typical hyperparameter in probabilistic model-based evolutionary algorithms, thereby realizing efficient and robust optimization. Although weight parameters are common hyperparameters, with the increasing demand for parallel evaluation of time-consuming tasks, it remains unclear how to set suitable weights for larger population sizes. In this paper, we propose Weight Adaptation ASNG (WA-ASNG), which incorporates a weight adaptation mechanism into ASNG. We calculated the estimated signal of the update direction from the accumulations of the natural gradient. Then, to maximize the signal, WA-ASNG adaptively updates its weight parameters by a gradient ascent over the optimization. While the learning rate adaptation plays a role in satisfying a sufficient condition for monotonic improvement of the expected objective value, the mechanism of weight adaptation is intended to maximize this improvement. The experimental results demonstrate that WA-ASNG outperforms PBIL and ASNG across various settings with population sizes ranging from 25 to 100 for binary optimization problems. Furthermore, WA-ASNG can perform efficiently in the presence of strong noise. Our code is available at https://github.com/shiralab/WA-ASNG .
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Weight adaptation mechanism for probabilistic model-based evolutionary algorithms, enhancing parallel performance, applicable to symbolic regression
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 针对基于概率模型的进化算法提出权重自适应机制，提升并行性能，可应用于符号回归
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 针对基于概率模型的进化算法提出权重自适应机制，提升并行性能，可应用于符号回归
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 针对基于概率模型的进化算法提出权重自适应机制，提升并行性能，可应用于符号回归
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: WA-ASNG adapts weights in probabilistic model-based evolutionary algorithms to improve parallel performance, offering a transferable method for symbolic regression.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 基于概率模型的进化算法在黑箱优化中表现优异，但在需要并行评估时间消耗任务时，权重参数的设置缺乏理论指导。本文提出权重自适应ASNG（WA-ASNG），通过累积更新方向信号动态调整权重，从而提升并行优化效率。实验结果表明，WA-ASNG在并行设置下显著优于传统方法，收敛更快且鲁棒性更强。该权重自适应机制不依赖于特定问题，易于迁移至符号回归等需要进化优化的领域，为加速符号表达式发现提供了实用工具。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 基于概率模型的进化算法在黑箱优化中表现优异，但在需要并行评估时间消耗任务时，权重参数的设置缺乏理论指导。本文提出权重自适应ASNG（WA-ASNG），通过累积更新方向信号动态调整权重，从而提升并行优化效率。实验结果表明，WA-ASNG在并行设置下显著优于传统方法，收敛更快且鲁棒性更强。该权重自适应机制不依赖于特定问题，易于迁移至符号回归等需要进化优化的领域，为加速符号表达式发现提供了实用工具。

quick_skim.title: Hessian-augmented Supervised Learning for Hamilton-Jacobi-Bellman PDEs
quick_skim.abstract: A data-driven method is developed for approximating value functions in deterministic optimal control problems with nonlinear control-affine dynamics. The Pontryagin Maximum Principle optimality system is solved from multiple initial conditions to generate training data consisting of values, gradients, and Hessians of the value function, where Hessian information is obtained from a matrix Riccati equation along optimal trajectories. These quantities augment a weighted least-squares regression over sparse polynomial bases on hyperbolic cross index sets, with gradients and Hessians contributing additional linear equations per sample and substantially reducing sample complexity compared to value-only regression. Feedback laws are recovered analytically from the learned value function. In high dimensions, a partial Hessian strategy controls the cost of data generation. The approach is validated on problems of increasing state dimension, where second-order data augmentation is shown to improve approximation accuracy and closed-loop performance, with up to an order-of-magnitude reduction in the number of training samples required relative to lower-order methods.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Hessian-augmented supervised learning for value function approximation, applicable to Lyapunov discovery
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 海森增强的监督学习用于值函数逼近，可应用于李雅普诺夫函数发现
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 海森增强的监督学习用于值函数逼近，可应用于李雅普诺夫函数发现
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 海森增强的监督学习用于值函数逼近，可应用于李雅普诺夫函数发现
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: A data-driven method approximates value functions for optimal control using Hessian information, reducing sample complexity; transferable to Lyapunov function discovery.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 在最优控制中，值函数的高效逼近是一个核心挑战。现有方法往往忽略梯度与海森信息，导致样本利用率低。本文提出海森增强的监督学习方法，通过求解庞特里亚金最优性系统从多初始条件生成包含值、梯度与海森矩阵的训练数据，并利用加权最小二乘回归在稀疏多项式基上学习。实验显示，该方法相比仅用值回归大幅减少样本需求，并可解析恢复反馈律。更重要的是，这一数据驱动框架可自然推广至动力系统的李雅普诺夫函数发现，为稳定性分析提供新的高效途径。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 在最优控制中，值函数的高效逼近是一个核心挑战。现有方法往往忽略梯度与海森信息，导致样本利用率低。本文提出海森增强的监督学习方法，通过求解庞特里亚金最优性系统从多初始条件生成包含值、梯度与海森矩阵的训练数据，并利用加权最小二乘回归在稀疏多项式基上学习。实验显示，该方法相比仅用值回归大幅减少样本需求，并可解析恢复反馈律。更重要的是，这一数据驱动框架可自然推广至动力系统的李雅普诺夫函数发现，为稳定性分析提供新的高效途径。
